perturbation.original_text: 1. Purpose . Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Parties may each find it necessa ry to provide to each other certain Confidential Information (as such term is defined herein) in connection with the Business Purpose . As a condition to either Party furnishing such Confidential Information, the Parties hereby agree to treat such Confidential Information provided to the other Party (the “Receiving Party” or “Recipient”) by or on behalf of the other Party (the “Disclosing Party”) in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and to take or abstain from taking certain other actions herein set forth.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Purpose . Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Parties may each find it necessa ry to provide to each other certain Confidential Information (as such term is not specifically defined herein) in connection with the Business Purpose . As a condition to either Party furnishing such Confidential Information, the Parties hereby agree to treat such Confidential Information provided to the other Party (the “Receiving Party” or “Recipient”) by or on behalf of the other Party (the “Disclosing Party”) in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and to take or abstain from taking certain other actions herein set forth.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 1, where 'as such term is defined herein' is replaced with 'as such term is not specifically defined herein', creates an in-text contradiction with Section 7, which in fact does define "Confidential Information". Because Section 1 is meant to introduce the NDA, but the term is defined later on. This creates an issue in whether Confidential Information is actually something within the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Definition s. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Confidential Information” shall mean any information furnished to the Receiving Party or its Representatives, whether oral, written or visual, and regardless of the manner in which it is furnished, by or on behalf of the Disclosing Party or its Representative, and shall include , without limitation: a) information constituting a "Trade S ecret", as defined in Idaho Code, Title 48, Chapter 8; Idaho Trade Secrets Act, as amended from time to time, and will include technical or non -technical data, a formal, a pattern, a compilation, a program, a software program, a device, a method, a technique, a drawing, a process, financial data, financial plans, product plans, non -public forecasts, studies, projections, analyses, all customer data of any kind, or a list of actual or potential customers or suppliers, business and contractual relationships, or any information similar to the foregoing which (i) derives economic value, ac tual or potential, from not being generally known and not readily ascertainable by proper means to other persons who can obtain economic value from its disclosure or use; and (ii) is the subject of efforts that are reasonable under the circumstances to ma intain its secrecy. Trade Secrets will include any information meeting the foregoing criteria which belongs to third parties and which the Disclosing Party under this Agreement is obligated to hold in confidence; b) the non -public fina ncial statements of a p arty; c) any confidential and proprietary information which the Disclosing P arty obtains from another person or entity under an obligati on of confidence and which the Disclosing P arty treats as proprietary; and d) any valuable, secret b usiness information, other than Trade Secrets, including but not limited to all specifications, drawings, sketches, models, samples, reports, plans, forecasts, and all other technical, financial or business data that is designated or identified as confidential at the time of disclo sure or by its nature is clearly recognizable as confidential information to a reasonably prudent person with knowledge of the Disclosing Party’s business and industry
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Confidential Information' is defined within the agreement, while the changed text states that it is 'not specifically defined herein'. This directly contradicts section 7, which provides a detailed definition of 'Confidential Information'. The change creates an in-text contradiction because section 1 introduces the agreement pertaining to Confidential Information. By changing the original text to state that it is not specifically defined, the modification introduces ambiguity and conflict within the contract, making the modified text a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Continuing Obligation . The confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall re main in effect for the longer of (i) five (5) years after the termination of this Agreement, or (ii) five (5) years after the expiration or termina tion of th e Business Purpose. Notwithstanding the foregoing, with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a Trade Secret, the confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall survive as long as the information disclosed continues to constitute a T rade Secret under applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Continuing Obligation . The confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall re main in effect for the shorter of (i) one (1) year after the termination of this Agreement, or (ii) one (1) year after the expiration or termina tion of th e Business Purpose. Notwithstanding the foregoing, with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a Trade Secret, the confidentiality obligations under this Agreement shall not survive termination or expiration of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the duration of confidentiality obligations from 'the longer of five years' to 'the shorter of one year', and removes the perpetual protection for trade secrets. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 9 (Equitable Remedy), as the original obligation was meant to be enforced in perpetuity in the case of Trade Secret. In this case, that provision no longer exists, which causes confusion in enforcement of Equitable Remedy
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Equitable Remedy . The Parties acknowledge and agree that: (i) the covenants in this Agreement are reasonable under the circumstances and are necessary to protect the Parties and the property of such Parties; and (ii) the breach by one Party of any of the provisions of this Agreement would cause serious and irreparable harm to the other Party that could not be adequately remedied by monetary damages alone. Each Party, therefore, consents to an order specifically enforcing the provisions of this Agreement or an order being issued against such Party enjoining or restraining it from any breach of the provisions of this Agreement, and agrees that suc h orders may be issued against such Party without the necessity of an undertaking as to damages or posting of bond by the Party seeking such orders. T he Provisions of this Section 9 shall not derogate from any other remedy available at law or in equity tha t one Party may be entitled in the event of breach hereunder by the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 6 to significantly reduce the duration of confidentiality obligations and eliminate perpetual protection for trade secrets. This directly contradicts Section 9, which provides for equitable remedies, including specific performance and injunctive relief, to protect the parties' property, especially given the parties agreeing that monetary damages alone would not be adequate in the case of breach. The elimination of perpetual protection for trade secrets, as per the modification in Section 6, undermines the premise upon which Section 9's equitable remedies are based, creating a strong contradiction and making enforcement of Section 9 unclear and difficult.

perturbation.original_text: 14. Modification . Except as permitted in Section 21 below (unilaterally changing its address), this Agreement may be modified, amended, superseded, or cancelled only by a written in strument signed by each of the P arties hereto, and any of the terms, covenants, representat ions, warranties or conditions hereof may be waived only by a written instrument executed by the party to be bound by any such waiver.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Modification . Except as permitted in Section 21 below (unilaterally changing its address), this Agreement may be modified orally by mutual agreement of authorized representatives of the Parties. Any of the terms, covenants, representat ions, warranties or conditions hereof may be waived only by a written instrument executed by the party to be bound by any such waiver.
perturbation.explanation: The modification allows for oral modifications of the agreement, which directly contradicts the original clause requiring written amendments. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 13 states that this is the Entire Agreement between parties, and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements. Allowing an oral agreement, makes Section 13 no longer reliable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement and understanding of the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, arrangements, and understandings with respect thereto. No representati on, promise, inducement, statement or intention has been made by any party hereto that is not embodied herein, and no party shall be bound by or liable for any alleged representation, promise, inducement, or statement not so set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 14 to allow oral modifications of the agreement, which directly contradicts Section 13 stating that the agreement supersedes all prior oral agreements and represents the entire agreement. The original text of Section 14 allowed for modification only by written instrument. By allowing oral modifications, the changed text introduces a clear inconsistency with Section 13, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it violates the entire agreement clause.

perturbation.original_text: 22. Successors and Assigns . This Agreement and its rights and obligations shall not be assignable without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, and any attempted assignment without such consent shall be void; provided , however , that such consent shall not be require d in the case of an assignment to a successor to the business and assets of the assignor, which successor agrees to be bound to the terms of this Agreement and the obligations of the assignor under this Agreement. Subject to the preceding sentence, the pr ovisions of this Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and shall be binding upon the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns. An assigning party shall continue to be bound to all of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 22. Successors and Assigns . This Agreement and its rights and obligations shall not be assignable without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, and any attempted assignment without such consent shall be void; provided , however , that such consent shall not be require d in the case of an assignment to a successor to the business and assets of the assignor, which successor automatically assumes all terms of this agreement without needing to agree to be bound. Subject to the preceding sentence, the pr ovisions of this Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and shall be binding upon the parties hereto and their respective successors and assigns. An assigning party shall continue to be bound to all of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modification removes the requirement for the successor to 'agree to be bound to the terms of this Agreement'. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 1, where both parties are agreeing to the NDA. If the successor is not agreeing to the NDA, it goes against the original intent of the purpose of Section 1 of both parties agreeing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Purpose . Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Parties may each find it necessa ry to provide to each other certain Confidential Information (as such term is defined herein) in connection with the Business Purpose . As a condition to either Party furnishing such Confidential Information, the Parties hereby agree to treat such Confidential Information provided to the other Party (the “Receiving Party” or “Recipient”) by or on behalf of the other Party (the “Disclosing Party”) in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and to take or abstain from taking certain other actions herein set forth.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 22, removing the requirement for the successor to agree to be bound by the terms of the agreement. This directly contradicts Section 1, which states that both parties agree to treat confidential information according to the terms of the agreement. If the successor automatically assumes the terms without agreeing, it undermines the mutual agreement required by Section 1, creating a strong contradiction.
